question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to any arbitrary topicId (e.g., a high-value HCS topic used for payment confirmations) by crafting a TopicMessageFilter with that topicId, causing them to receive all financial settlement messages published on that topic and act on them before the legitimate recipient, leading to direct loss of funds through front-running?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all active financial HCS topics by iterating over numeric topicId values in the channel name format 'topic.%d', subscribing to each, and intercepting payment-confirmation messages intended for other users, leading to direct loss of funds through unauthorized access to financial message streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger unbounded resource exhaustion by opening thousands of concurrent subscriptions to distinct topicIds, causing the ConcurrentHashMap topicMessages to grow without bound and exhausting JVM heap, crashing the gRPC service and causing legitimate financial subscribers to miss critical payment-confirmation messages, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval) retry loop in subscribe() by repeatedly connecting and disconnecting to force infinite retry cycles that consume all threads in the boundedElastic scheduler, starving legitimate financial subscribers of message delivery and causing direct loss of funds through missed settlement confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Flux produced by computeIfAbsent() and share() to receive financial messages belonging to other subscribers on the same topic, since all subscribers to the same topicId share a single hot publisher, leading to cross-tenant financial data leakage and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a legitimate financial subscriber's stream to terminate by triggering a BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR condition in the onBackpressureBuffer() of SharedTopicListener, by flooding the shared topic with high-volume messages that exceed maxBufferSize (16384), causing the subscriber to miss critical payment messages and suffer direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user force a targeted unsubscribe() by cancelling their own subscription to a shared topic at a precise moment when other legitimate financial subscribers are relying on the same shared Flux, causing the shared Flux to terminate and all co-subscribers to lose their message stream, resulting in missed financial confirmations and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator on the subscribe() Flux to receive a replay of messages that were already delivered to other subscribers when the shared subscription is re-established after a retry, causing duplicate financial message processing and direct loss of funds through double-spend or double-settlement?
